title: Metal substrate provided with tailored surface textures and method for applying such textures on metal substrates
abstract: The invention relates to a metal substrate provided with surface textures wherein different texture patterns are provided over predefined surface areas of the metal substrate and wherein the different texture patterns are tailored to predefined surface properties of a product which is to be made out of the metal substrate as well as to a method for applying such surface textures on the metal substrate.
claims:
Claims 1. Metal substrate provided with surface textures characterised in that different texture patterns are provided over the surface area of the metal substrate wherein the texture pattern comprise dimples with a diameter in a range of 25 - 120 mm, a depth in a range of 2 -12 mm and a density in a range of 100 to 10000 per square millimetre. 2. Metal substrate according to claim 1 , wherein the texture patterns are optimised for one or more properties from a group of properties comprising: paint properties, visual appearance properties, friction properties, scratch resistance properties, anti-stick properties, repellent properties. 3. Metal substrate according to claim 1 or 2, wherein between texture patterns a transition texture pattern is applied to provide a transition between texture patterns. 4. Metal substrate according to any of the previous claims, wherein the spacing between dimples is different in predefined directions. 5. Metal substrate according to any of the previous claims, wherein the texture patterns have a roughness Ra in a range of 0.2 - 3 mm, a peak count RPc in a range of 30 - 190 per centimetre and a waviness of at most 0.35 mm 6. Metal substrate according to any of the previous claims, wherein the substrate is provided with reference points and wherein applied texture patterns are positioned with respect of the reference points. 7. Metal substrate according to any of the previous claims, wherein the metal substrate is a blank or a strip. 8. Method for applying surface textures on a metal substrate characterised in that the method comprises the steps of: collecting data concerning dimensions, shape, forming operation and predefined surface properties of a product which is to be made out of the metal substrate, applying one or more texture patterns on the metal substrate wherein the 

 texture patterns are applied on basis of the collected data such that the product has the predefined surface properties. 9. Method according to claim 8, wherein the texture pattern is optimised for one or more properties from a group of properties comprising: paint properties, visual appearance properties, friction properties, scratch resistance properties, anti-stick properties, repellent properties. 10. Method according to claim 8 or 9, wherein between texture patterns a transition texture pattern is applied to provide a transition between texture patterns. 1 1. Method according to any of claims 8-10, wherein a texture pattern tailored for paint properties has a roughness Ra in a range of 0.2 - 3 mm, a peak count RPc in a range of 30 - 190 per centimetre and a waviness of at most 0.35 mm. 12. Method according to any of claims 8-10, wherein a texture pattern tailored for paint properties has a roughness Ra in a range of 0.8 - 1.4 mm, a peak count RPc in a range of 75 - 1 10 per centimetre and a waviness of at most 0.29 mm. 13. Method according to claim 11 or 12, wherein the texture patterns comprise dimples with a diameter in a range of 25 - 120 mm, a depth in a range of 2 - 12 mm and a density in a range of 100 to 10000 per square millimetre. 14. Method according to claim 11 or 12, wherein the texture patterns comprise dimples with a diameter in a range of 35 - 60 mm, a depth in a range of 3 - 5 mm and a density in a range of 100 - 500 per square millimetre. 15. Method according to any of claims 8-10, wherein a texture pattern tailored for friction properties has a roughness Ra, a peak count RPc and a waviness according to the values for paint appearance in claims 1 1 , 12 and with dimples with a diameter and depth according claims 13, 14 a dimple density in a range of 100 - 360 per square millimetre. 16. Method according to any of claims 8-15, wherein the spacing between dimples is varied dependent on deformation data. 17. Method according to any of claims 8-16, wherein the spacing between dimples is varied dependent on the position in which paint is applied on the product.
